skill.name: techdoc-search-and-orchestrate
skill.description: Routes technical documentation questions to a dedicated Context7 research agent and chooses sequential or parallel lookup patterns.
skill.summary: Delegate documentation research to specialized agents while keeping the main conversation focused.

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged backticks, DES-like substrings, and high entropy, but the reviewed file is a Markdown orchestration guide. No shell execution, weak cryptography, binary payload, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found. The residual risk is low because it depends on a named subagent and Context7 workflow.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks mark inline tool names and fenced examples for Task tool parameters. They do not execute commands or pass user input to a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content is Markdown prose and examples only. I found no shell command syntax, script file, interpreter call, or command execution API.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern from Description Fields
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography hits align with frontmatter and Task parameter names such as description. No encryption algorithm or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched locations are labels and natural-language descriptions. I found no DES, MD5, cipher selection, hashing code, or credential handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy from Multilingual Markdown
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The file is Chinese Markdown text with examples. No encoded blob, binary content, packed script, or hidden payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entire file is readable Markdown with frontmatter, headings, and examples. The high entropy score is consistent with multilingual text, not obfuscation.

content.value_statement: Technical documentation queries can flood the main conversation with long search results. This skill delegates research to Context7-focused agents and returns concise findings.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when a request needs technical documentation research.
Routes documentation lookups to a context7-researcher agent.
Recommends parallel searches for independent documentation topics.
Recommends sequential searches when one answer depends on another.
Provides examples for API usage, configuration, error analysis, and version differences.
limitations: Requires a configured context7-researcher agent to perform the actual lookup.
Does not include its own documentation index or retrieval code.
Does not verify results beyond the delegated research workflow.
Focused on technical documentation, not general web research.

use_cases.title: Research API Usage
use_cases.description: Route a library API question to a specialized documentation agent and receive a focused usage summary.

use_cases.title: Compare Framework Versions
use_cases.description: Delegate version difference research for frameworks such as Next.js and summarize the important changes.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multiple Lookups
use_cases.description: Split independent documentation questions across parallel research agents for faster answers.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up One API
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single documentation answer.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the documentation research workflow to find how [library] implements [feature]. Include the main API and a short usage explanation.

prompt_templates.title: Find Configuration Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for setup and parameters.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [framework] configuration for [goal]. Return the required settings, optional parameters, and common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Independent Topics
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with multiple unrelated documentation topics.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research these independent topics in parallel: [topic one], [topic two], and [topic three]. Summarize each result separately.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Error Then Resolve
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request where the fix depends on the error cause.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [error message] in [technology]. First identify likely causes, then research recommended fixes for the most likely cause.

output_examples.input: How does Supabase realtime integrate with a JavaScript app?
output_examples.output: A concise summary of the relevant Supabase realtime documentation, key API calls, setup requirements, and a usage outline.

output_examples.input: Compare Next.js App Router setup and Server Actions usage.
output_examples.output: Two separate research summaries, one for App Router configuration and one for Server Actions behavior and constraints.

output_examples.input: What causes a Next.js ENOENT error and how should I fix it?
output_examples.output: A staged answer that first explains common causes, then lists targeted fixes based on the documented cause.

best_practices: Use parallel research only when topics are independent.
Use sequential research when later answers depend on earlier findings.
Ask for source-specific summaries when accuracy matters.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for nontechnical general research.
Do not split one tightly coupled investigation into unrelated agents.
Do not treat delegated research as a substitute for final answer review.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It routes technical documentation questions to a Context7-focused research agent and organizes the lookup workflow.

faq.question: Does it execute shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is Markdown guidance for agent delegation and contains no executable scripts.

faq.question: Does it require Context7?
faq.answer: Yes. It assumes a context7-researcher agent that can use Context7 documentation tools.

faq.question: When should I use parallel research?
faq.answer: Use it when documentation topics are independent and do not need results from each other.

faq.question: When should I use sequential research?
faq.answer: Use it when the first lookup determines what the next lookup should ask.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only false positives and no confirmed malicious behavior.
